title: What's your name?
pedagogy: Vocabulary: อะไร (mid อ + dead short ะ → low; low ร + live ไ◌ → mid). Concept: question words sit in the answer's slot — no word-order inversion (คุณชื่ออะไร = you-name-what). Question particle review: female questions end in high-tone คะ (greet-01 minimal pair), male ครับ is invariant.

exercises.title: Questions don't rearrange anything
exercises.thaiExample: คุณ · ชื่อ · อะไร
body: English flips words around to ask a question. Thai doesn't move a thing — the question word simply sits where the answer will go.
คุณชื่ออะไร is literally “you – are-named – WHAT”. Answer: ผมชื่อมาร์ค — มาร์ค lands exactly where อะไร was.
That slot rule works for every question word you'll meet: what, where, when, how much.

exercises.gloss: you + be-named + what + male polite particle
exercises.explanation: Statement order, question word in the answer slot, particle at the end. ครับ stays ครับ for questions and statements alike.

exercises.context: You're a woman asking a new colleague her name.
exercises.question: How does the question end?

choices.thai: คุณชื่ออะไรคะ
คุณชื่ออะไรค่ะ
คุณชื่ออะไรครับ

exercises.explanation: A question from a female speaker ends in high-tone คะ — the greet-01 minimal pair doing real work. Falling ค่ะ is for statements; ครับ is the male particle.

exercises.gloss: you + be-named + what + female question particle
exercises.explanation: Hear the high tone on the final คะ? That's the question particle — its falling twin ค่ะ was sitting in the bank as a trap.
